## SECTION 4 — India National Updates
The incredibly massive, deeply humiliating hostage crisis in southern India, having finally and successfully concluded with the aggressive release of Dr. Rajkumar, heavily shifted into a massive political post-mortem. While absolute, massive relief swept across Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, the state governments faced incredibly fierce, massive public outrage and intense media scrutiny. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On November 21, 1995, the incredibly massive, highly historic, and deeply consequential Dayton Agreement was officially and aggressively initialed at the Wright-Patterson Air Force Base in Ohio. Brokered heavily by the United States, this incredibly massive diplomatic treaty absolutely and formally ended the horrific, deeply bloody, and massive three-and-a-half-year Bosnian War. The massive agreement fundamentally and aggressively partitioned Bosnia and Herzegovina into two incredibly highly autonomous entities, heavily enforcing a massive, deeply complex peace through the massive deployment of NATO peacekeeping forces.

On November 21, 1783, the incredibly massive, incredibly daring, and historically defining absolute first untethered human flight was successfully and aggressively achieved in Paris. This incredibly massive, absolutely unprecedented technological triumph fundamentally shattered the absolute, earthly confinement of humanity, fiercely launching the incredibly massive, modern era of global aviation and aerospace exploration.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On November 21, 1962, the incredibly massive, heavily devastating, and deeply traumatic Sino-Indian War absolutely and officially concluded. Following a massive, incredibly rapid, and overwhelming military advance deep into massive Indian territory in the Himalayas, the Chinese People's Liberation Army suddenly and aggressively declared a massive, unilateral ceasefire. China heavily and aggressively withdrew its massive forces to the pre-war Line of Actual Control. The incredibly massive, humiliating military defeat absolutely shattered Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and fundamentally forced a massive, aggressive modernization of the entire Indian military apparatus.
